jobTitle: W2 professorship with career track to W3 in Modern German Literature (f/m/d)
jobDescription: The Faculty of Philosophy of the University of Erfurt sees itself as an academic teaching and research institution that both takes on and further develops new approaches in the humanities and cultural studies within the framework of a research-oriented and inter-national university. The advertised professorship is part of the Seminar for Literary Studies at the University of Erfurt. This Seminar offers a transphilological structure that is unique in Thuringia while being open to the world, and in which the individual 'na-tional philologies' work closely together on a theory- and project-oriented basis.
jobTasks: excellent achievements in research and teaching in the field of Modern German Literature, proven, among other things, by a habilitation or other additional achievements in the field of Modern German Literature
a research focus on media-studies related perspectives on literature
the willingness to participate in interdisciplinary research areas
a high level of commitment to teaching, the willingness and ability to acquire third-party funding and to participate in academic self-administration
in teaching, the ability to represent the subject in all its breadth while also making reference to the contemporary world
a research focus on transculturality, e.g. literature and culture of post-migrant society
international experience in research or teaching
